desc: Kutaisi is western Georgia's most practical heritage-and-nature base, combining UNESCO sites in town with easy access to canyon walks, caves, and mountain day trips in Imereti. It suits travelers who want active excursions without Tbilisi-level prices or pacing.
hls: Compact old town around the Rioni with easy walk-and-café rhythm
UNESCO Bagrati Cathedral and Gelati Monastery heritage anchors
Canyon and cave network day trips (Martvili, Okatse, Prometheus)
Good launch point for Svaneti/Racha overland itineraries
Generally lower accommodation pressure than Georgia's capital
Strong shoulder-season value for mixed city-nature trips

todo.name: Bagrati + Gelati heritage circuit
todo.desc: Start at Bagrati Cathedral for city views, then continue to Gelati Monastery complex for frescoes and monastic history; combine with Motsameta if road conditions and timing allow.

todo.name: Okatse Canyon trail + viewpoints
todo.desc: Plan a half-day around the marked forest approach and suspended canyon walkway (conditions permitting); wear grippy shoes because wet wooden sections can be slippery after rain.

todo.name: Martvili Canyon boat and rim walk
todo.desc: Use an early slot to avoid queues in peak months, then pair short canyon walks with nearby village lunch stops for a low-strain but high-scenery day.

todo.name: Prometheus Cave and Sataplia pairing
todo.desc: Combine underground chambers at Prometheus with dinosaur-footprint exhibits and forest viewpoints at Sataplia Reserve to create a weather-resilient excursion day.

todo.name: Tskaltubo sanatorium photography loop
todo.desc: Visit selected accessible former spa buildings with a guide for context and safety, then return to Kutaisi for evening dining around David Agmashenebeli Square.

todo.name: Racha or Upper Svaneti long day
todo.desc: If conditions are stable, book a private/driver-guided mountain extension for passes and river-valley viewpoints; treat as full-day with early departure and conservative return planning.

wifi.note: Central Kutaisi hotels and cowork-friendly cafés are usually dependable; speeds can drop during power or weather disruptions in outer districts.

prac.fltNote: Kutaisi (KUT) has low-cost links from several European cities but no consistent nonstop from London Gatwick year-round. UK trips are often one-stop or via Tbilisi; return fares are frequently ~£120-£260 off-peak, ~£260-£420 in shoulder demand, and higher in late-summer holiday peaks.
prac.currency: Georgian lari (GEL); cards are common in town but canyon/roadside stops often require cash.
prac.lang: Georgian is primary; English is improving in tourism services but less consistent outside core areas.
prac.visa: Georgia is visa-free for many passports for generous stays, but verify nationality-specific limits and overland transit requirements.
alerts: Canyon walkways and cave operations can close temporarily after heavy rain or maintenance notices.
Mountain road-day trips require buffer time for landslides, fog, or construction delays.
Carry layers: spring/autumn mornings are cool even when afternoons are warm.

sweet: Late April-June and September-October are usually best for canyon access, moderate temperatures, and smoother road logistics than midsummer storm periods or winter closures.
